summary: Weak static pattern match in auth guard; no corroborating evidence of installer harm
details: A pattern rule matched on keyword co-occurrence (the tokens 'ping', 'GET', and 'id') within dist/guards/auth.guard.js of common-tg-service@1.3.235. The matched tokens are typical of a NestJS authentication guard (HTTP method strings, identifier fields, health-check terminology) and do not, on their own, demonstrate exfiltration or command execution. No hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint, credential read from installer-owned paths, lifecycle-hook execution, or dropper behavior was identified in the flagged code. Without concrete evidence tying the matched tokens to a data-out or code-in mechanism against the installer, the signal is a low-confidence keyword co-occurrence that warrants human review rather than a public block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
